message:
refactor(web): redesign landing page for utility-first UX

- Remove marketing bloat: testimonials, pricing, features sections
- Compact Hero: faster animations (0.3s vs 2s), remove orbs, remove CTAs
- Add FeaturedPromptsSection: 6 prompts visible immediately
- Add minimal footer with subtle Pro badge
- Prompts now visible within first viewport

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Counter-Notice - Allow content owner to dispute - Required counter-notice format - 10-14 day waiting period - Restore if no lawsuit filed ### 3. Repeat Infringers - Track strike count - 3 strikes policy - Account termination - Appeals process ## Submission Form Fields ```typescript interface DMCARequest { // Claimant info claimantName: string; claimantEmail: string; claimantAddress: string; // Content info copyrightedWorkDescription: string; copyrightedWorkUrl?: string; infringingContentUrl: string; // Legal attestations goodFaithStatement: boolean; accuracyStatement: boolean; ownershipStatement: boolean; // Signature signature: string; signatureDate: Date; } ``` ## Database ```typescript export const dmcaRequests = pgTable('dmca_requests', { id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(), // Claimant claimantName: text('claimant_name').notNull(), claimantEmail: text('claimant_email').notNull(), // Content contentType: text('content_type').notNull(), contentId: uuid('content_id').notNull(), contentOwnerId: uuid('content_owner_id').references(() => userProfiles.id), // Status status: text('status').default('pending'), // pending, removed, restored, dismissed // Counter-notice counterNoticeAt: timestamp('counter_notice_at'), counterNoticeContent: text('counter_notice_content'), // Resolution resolvedAt: timestamp('resolved_at'), resolvedBy: uuid('resolved_by'), resolution: text('resolution'), createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(), }); export const copyrightStrikes = pgTable('copyright_strikes', { id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(), userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(), dmcaRequestId: uuid('dmca_request_id').references(() => dmcaRequests.id), reason: text('reason').notNull(), issuedAt: timestamp('issued_at').defaultNow(), expiresAt: timestamp('expires_at'), // Strikes may expire after time }); ``` ## Workflow 1. It provides a seamless experience: 1. User runs `jfp login` 2. Browser opens automatically 3. User clicks "Sign in with Google" 4. CLI automatically receives token 5. Done! ## File: `packages/cli/src/commands/login.ts` ### Implementation ```typescript import { createServer, type IncomingMessage, type ServerResponse } from "http"; import { URL } from "url"; import open from "open"; import chalk from "chalk"; import boxen from "boxen"; import { saveCredentials, loadCredentials, type Credentials } from "../lib/credentials"; const PREMIUM_URL = process.env.JFP_PREMIUM_URL ?? "https://premium.jeffreysprompts.com"; const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 120_000; // 2 minutes interface LoginOptions { remote?: boolean; timeout?: number; } export async function loginCommand(options: LoginOptions = {}): Promise { // Check if already logged in const existing = await loadCredentials(); if (existing) { console.log(chalk.yellow("You are already logged in as " + existing.email)); console.log(chalk.dim("Run 'jfp logout' first to switch accounts")); return; } // Force remote flow if requested or if no display available if (options.remote || !canOpenBrowser()) { return loginRemote(options); } return loginLocal(options); } function canOpenBrowser(): boolean { // Check if we're in an environment where we can open a browser // SSH sessions typically don't have DISPLAY set if (process.env.SSH_CLIENT || process.env.SSH_TTY) { return false; } // Check for display on Linux if (process.platform === "linux" && !process.env.DISPLAY && !process.env.WAYLAND_DISPLAY) { return false; } return true; } async function loginLocal(options: LoginOptions): Promise { const timeout = options.timeout ?? DEFAULT_TIMEOUT; console.log(chalk.dim("Starting login flow...")); // Start local server to receive callback const { port, tokenPromise, close } = await startCallbackServer(timeout); // Build auth URL const authUrl = new URL(`${PREMIUM_URL}/cli/auth`); authUrl.searchParams.set("port", String(port)); authUrl.searchParams.set("redirect", "local"); console.log(chalk.dim("\nOpening browser to sign in with Google...")); console.log(chalk.dim(`URL: ${authUrl.toString()}\n`)); // Open browser try { await open(authUrl.toString()); } catch { console.log(chalk.yellow("Could not open browser automatically.")); console.log(`Please visit: ${chalk.cyan(authUrl.toString())}`); } // Wait for callback console.log(chalk.dim("Waiting for authentication...")); try { const credentials = await tokenPromise; close(); // Save credentials await saveCredentials(credentials); console.log(boxen( `${chalk.green("✓")} Logged in as ${chalk.bold(credentials.email)}\n` + `Tier: ${chalk.cyan(credentials.tier)}`, { padding: 1, margin: 1, borderStyle: "round", borderColor: "green" } )); } catch (err) { close(); if (err instanceof Error && err.message === "timeout") { console.log(chalk.red("\nLogin timed out. Please try again.")); console.log(chalk.dim("Tip: Use 'jfp login --remote' for headless environments")); } else { console.log(chalk.red("\nLogin failed:"), err instanceof Error ? err.message : err); } process.exit(1); } } interface CallbackServer { port: number; tokenPromise: Promise; close: () => void; } async function startCallbackServer(timeoutMs: number): Promise { return new Promise((resolve, reject) => { let resolveToken: (creds: Credentials) => void; let rejectToken: (err: Error) => void; const tokenPromise = new Promise((res, rej) => { resolveToken = res; rejectToken = rej; }); const server = createServer((req: IncomingMessage, res: ServerResponse) => { const url = new URL(req.url!, `http://localhost`); if (url.pathname === "/callback") { // Parse token from query params const token = url.searchParams.get("token"); const email = url.searchParams.get("email"); const tier = url.searchParams.get("tier") as "free" | "premium"; const expiresAt = url.searchParams.get("expires_at"); const userId = url.searchParams.get("user_id"); const refreshToken = url.searchParams.get("refresh_token"); const error = url.searchParams.get("error"); if (error) { res.writeHead(200, { "Content-Type": "text/html" }); res.end(errorPage(error)); rejectToken(new Error(error)); return; } if (!token || !email || !tier || !userId) { res.writeHead(400, { "Content-Type": "text/html" }); res.end(errorPage("Invalid callback parameters")); rejectToken(new Error("Invalid callback parameters")); return; } // Success! res.writeHead(200, { "Content-Type": "text/html" }); res.end(successPage(email)); resolveToken({ access_token: token, refresh_token: refreshToken ?? undefined, expires_at: expiresAt ?? new Date(Date.now() + 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString(), email, tier, user_id: userId, }); } else { res.writeHead(404); res.end("Not found"); } }); // Find available port server.listen(0, "127.0.0.1", () => { const addr = server.address(); const port = typeof addr === "object" && addr ? addr.port : 0; // Set timeout const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => { rejectToken(new Error("timeout")); }, timeoutMs); resolve({ port, tokenPromise: tokenPromise.finally(() => clearTimeout(timeoutId)), close: () => server.close(), }); }); server.on("error", reject); }); } function successPage(email: string): string { return ` Login Successful
✓

Login Successful!

Signed in as ${email}

You can close this window and return to the terminal.

`; } function errorPage(error: string): string { return ` Login Failed
✕

Login Failed

${error}
